name: Chesterfield-Colonial Heights Alliance for Social Ministry (CCHASM)
desc: Coalition of faith communities that coordinates emergency food-pantry appointments and utility and financial assistance for residents in Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and the Tri-Cities.

name: Chesterfield-Colonial Heights Christmas Mother Program, Inc.
desc: Volunteer-run program providing new toys, books, clothing and food gift cards to qualifying children, families and seniors in Chesterfield and Colonial Heights for the holidays.

name: Better Housing Coalition
desc: Builds and manages affordable homes and runs resident-services programs at properties across the Richmond region including Chesterfield.
address: 2108 W. Laburnum Ave., Suite 200, Richmond, VA 23227

name: CARITAS
desc: Operates an emergency homeless shelter, recovery housing and a furniture bank for single adults across greater Richmond including Chesterfield residents.

name: Homeward
desc: Regional planning and coordination nonprofit for homelessness services that operates the Greater Richmond Continuum of Care and a housing crisis line.

name: Commonwealth Catholic Charities (CCC)
desc: Provides broad human services including housing-resource navigation, daytime shelter support, refugee resettlement, immigration legal aid and case management.

name: Mercy Mall of Virginia
desc: Connects families in crisis to free essentials such as clothing and household items along with community resources.

name: Chesterfield-Colonial Heights Department of Social Services
desc: County and Colonial Heights public agency for Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, energy and fuel assistance and family services.

name: Chesterfield Food Bank Outreach Center (CFBOC)
desc: The largest food pantry in Chesterfield County, providing emergency food plus rotating weekly distributions across the county.

name: Feed More
desc: Central Virginia's regional food bank, Community Kitchen and Meals on Wheels, supplying a network of partner pantries across the region including Chesterfield.

name: Little Hands Virginia
desc: Collects and distributes new and gently used baby and child essentials for disadvantaged young children through partner agencies.

name: CrossOver Healthcare Ministry
desc: Provides free and sliding-scale healthcare for the uninsured and medically underserved, including primary care, dental, vision, pharmacy and behavioral health.

name: Daily Planet Health Services
desc: Federally qualified health center providing medical, dental and behavioral health to all regardless of insurance, income or housing status.

name: Commonwealth Autism
desc: Provides advocacy, educational tools and employment services for autistic individuals, their families and caregivers.

name: Postpartum Support Virginia
desc: Offers support, education and referrals for maternal mental health and runs volunteer-led support groups.

name: SOAR365
desc: Provides lifelong services for children and adults with disabilities, including therapy, day support, employment and recreation programs.

name: Sportable
desc: Runs adaptive and Paralympic sports programs for children, adults and veterans with physical and visual disabilities.

name: Justin J. Davis Heart Foundation
desc: Chesterfield-founded foundation that educates the community about heart conditions and supports cardiac health awareness.

name: Chesterfield CASA (Court Appointed Special Advocates)
desc: Trains and supports volunteers who advocate in court for children who have experienced abuse or neglect in Chesterfield County and Colonial Heights.
address: 7400 Beaufont Springs Dr., Suite 300, Richmond, VA 23225

name: Communities In Schools of Chesterfield
desc: Places coordinators inside Chesterfield public schools to connect at-risk students and families with resources, mentoring and support to stay in school.

name: Chesterfield Public Education Foundation (CEF)
desc: Raises private funds and runs grant, scholarship and partnership programs supporting Chesterfield County Public Schools students and teachers.

name: The L.E.E. Academy
desc: Provides supplemental academic instruction and enrichment to help students close learning gaps.

name: Start 1 Spark
desc: Helps under-resourced youth participate in extracurricular activities by covering fees and equipment.

name: Girls on the Run of Greater Richmond
desc: After-school program that uses running to build confidence and social-emotional skills in elementary and middle-school girls, with teams at Chesterfield schools.

name: Greater Richmond SCAN (Stop Child Abuse Now)
desc: Provides child abuse prevention and treatment through Child Advocacy Centers, family support, Richmond CASA and the Circle Preschool program.

name: YoungLives Metro Richmond
desc: Provides mentoring and support for teen moms and their children.

name: Comfort Zone Camp
desc: Runs free bereavement camps and support for grieving children and teens.

name: St. Joseph's Villa
desc: Provides education, crisis-stabilization, housing and family services for children and young people, including those with special needs.

name: YMCA of Greater Richmond
desc: Offers youth development, healthy living and community programs through branches serving Chesterfield.

name: Shepherd's Center of Chesterfield
desc: Volunteers provide free transportation, friendly visiting and lifelong-learning programs to help adults 60 and older stay independent at home.

name: Lucy Corr Foundation
desc: Fundraising arm of Lucy Corr, the nonprofit continuing-care retirement community in Chesterfield, supporting residents, day-care participants, staff and senior needs.

name: The Span Center
desc: The Area Agency on Aging for Central Virginia, providing care coordination, Meals on Wheels, caregiver support and benefits help for adults 60 and older including Chesterfield.

name: Chesterfield County Aging and Disability Resources
desc: County office coordinating services for adults 50 and older, people with disabilities and their caregivers.

name: Chesterfield County Humane Society, Inc.
desc: All-volunteer nonprofit that supplements Chesterfield County's animal shelter by funding spay/neuter, medical care and supplies to reduce euthanasia of adoptable animals.

name: Chesterfield County Animal Services
desc: The county's municipal animal shelter and adoption center.

name: Richmond SPCA
desc: No-kill humane society and adoption center whose service area includes Chesterfield, offering a low-cost veterinary clinic and community programs.

name: James River Association (JRA)
desc: Member-supported guardian of the James River conducting water-quality monitoring, restoration projects, advocacy and environmental education across the watershed.

name: James River Soil and Water Conservation District
desc: Works with farmers, homeowners and businesses in Chesterfield and Prince George to fund conservation practices that protect water and soil.

name: Perkinson Center for the Arts and Education
desc: Chesterfield's community arts center offering theater, a gallery, classes and performances in Chester.

name: Chesterfield Historical Society of Virginia
desc: Preserves and shares Chesterfield County history through a museum, archives, programs and the historic 1917 county jail and museum.

name: Friends of the Chesterfield County Public Library
desc: Volunteer support nonprofit that funds library programs, materials and literacy efforts through book sales and donations.

name: CultureWorks
desc: Regional arts-and-culture support organization providing grants, advocacy and shared services for the metro arts community.

name: Veteran Community Network of Central Virginia (VCNCV)
desc: Chesterfield-based network that connects veterans and families to local resources including behavioral health, housing, transition support and benefits navigation.

name: Virginians For Veterans (V4V)
desc: Richmond business-leader nonprofit that fundraises for veteran support groups such as Fisher House, Operation Healing Forces and Challenge America.

name: Chesterfield County Veterans Resources
desc: County page consolidating veterans benefits, services and contacts.

name: YWCA Richmond
desc: Provides domestic and sexual violence services for Richmond and Chesterfield, including a 24/7 regional hotline, emergency shelter, counseling and court and hospital advocacy.

name: Safe Harbor
desc: Provides comprehensive services for survivors of sexual and intimate-partner violence, including a 24-hour helpline, emergency shelter, counseling and court advocacy across greater Richmond.

name: Chesterfield County Domestic and Sexual Violence Resource Center
desc: County-based victim advocacy and crisis support for Chesterfield residents.

name: Recover Hope, Inc.
desc: Provides support and services for survivors of human trafficking.

name: Sacred Heart Center
desc: Hub of Richmond's Latino community offering ESL and GED in Spanish, immigration legal services, bilingual tax prep and family case management.

name: Latinos in Virginia Empowerment Center
desc: Provides bilingual advocacy and services for Latino survivors of domestic and sexual violence across Virginia.

name: Virginia Hispanic Chamber of Commerce & Foundation
desc: Provides business counseling, workforce development and the Passport to Education program for the Latino community.

name: Capaz+IT
desc: Provides career growth and technology and IT pathways for immigrant communities.

name: Substance Abuse Free Environment (SAFE)
desc: Chesterfield-based substance-use prevention organization providing education, coalitions and advocacy for youth and families.

name: C-Fit Community
desc: Provides healthy-living and wellness initiatives for the community.

name: FACES (Family Advocacy)
desc: Family-run advocacy and peer support organization focused on children's mental health.

name: SAARA of Virginia
desc: Provides substance-use recovery education, peer support and advocacy.

name: The Community Tax Law Project
desc: Provides free tax-dispute representation for low-income Virginians.

name: Capital Region Workforce Partnership / Virginia Career Works
desc: Provides employment and job-training services for the region including Chesterfield.
